title: quercus faginea var. prasina
intro: Quercus faginea var. prasina, also known as the Portuguese oak, is a subspecies of oak tree native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Quercus faginea var. prasina include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, or other remedies for various health conditions.
